videos.title: The 4 Mistakes People Make with Drinking Lemon Water (and Juice)

transcript.fullText: like to talk about four common mistakes that people make when they consume lemon water I've done lots of videos on lemon limes lemon water and the benefits but I really haven't talked about the mistakes that people make but the main benefit of lemon juice is what it can do for kidney stones in preventing kidney stones but the citrates interfere with the formation of a kidney stone okay and I'm talking about the calcium oxalates and I've done a video recently on that being related to a deficiency in citrate and so one way to get more citrates is to consume more lemon juice another benefit of lemon water or just lemon juice is for its vitamin C but just remember if you have pasteurized lemon juice the heat kills off the vitamin C now they probably add some vitamin C in the form of ascorbic acid which is not the four complex so if you really want to get the benefits of vitamin C from lemon juice or limes just take a lemon or a lime and then just squeeze it in water or blend the whole thing in a blender and you can actually drink that down and that works really good if you add a little bit of a like maybe electrolyte powder which has a little bit of stevia in there so it makes it a nice drink plus you have this Spike of electrolytes but if it's pasteurized chances are it's not going to have a lot of vitamin C but one of the common symptoms of vitamin C is bleeding gums so if you're brushing your teeth and you have this little pink toothbrush suspected vitamin C deficiency another symptom would be fatigue another effect from lemon juice is its antihistamine effect that's why it's good for allergies histamines are those things that your body makes in reaction to an allergy that causes some of the mucus and makes you tired there to some degree kind of like a fatigue poison another effect a lemon juice has is its anti-fungal properties so it's really good for candida fungus and bacterial infections as well so you can use it with other things as like a natural antibiotic another thing that lemon juice will do it will help prevent the formation of uric acid so it's good in gout some types of gout is called pseudogout which is really oxalates right going into the big toe it's not really uric acid and so citrates are really good to counter the effects of that effect and then also lemon juice can help increase the absorption of iron as well in the body all right so let's just dive right into the mistakes one is that you don't want to consume this lemon water right before you eat why because even though citric acid is an acid when it goes into the body it gets burned up and it turns into an alkaline substance okay so basically we're alkalizing this hydrochloric acid that we don't want to do and that's going to inhibit digestion so consume your lemon water at least maybe a half hour to an hour before you eat anything I consume my lemon juice in the morning when I'm fasting so there's no possibility of mixing it with a food mistake number two is you're just drinking this straight lemon juice just drinking it right out of the bottle whatever as a shot and you're allowing this very very strong acid to expose to your teeth okay I mean think about the pH of lemon juice is a pH of two to three that is similar to hydrochloric acid and so if you were to routinely do that and expose it to your teeth it could potentially kind of break down some of the enamel on your teeth so what I do is I dilute this lemon juice with water and I consume it with a straw okay that way it avoids going on my teeth all right mistake number three if you have an ulcer or gastritis which you'll know real quick because it's going to burn when you consume it it would be contraindicated you don't want to take anything that's acidic if you have this inflamed stomach or an ulcer okay so that would be one thing you would want to avoid but you'll probably very quickly recognize if you have these conditions because when you consume it it just it'll burn your stomach all right and last mistake is that if you have alkalosis right if you have alkalosis where your blood is just a little excessively alkaline I mean it's already alkaline slightly alkaline but if it's more alkaline and that's a condition called alkalosis and it can aggravate that pH and make it worse so a lot of the conditions or symptoms that occur when your body is too alkaline will get worse how do you know if you have alkalosis here are some common symptoms lightheadedness numbness and tingling in the extremities like your fingertips or feet tetany like a little twitching underneath the left eyelid or it could be anywhere in the body rapid shallow breathing okay like kind of a version of hyperventilating I mean think about when you hyperventilate right you're getting too much oxygen and you're creating a CO2 deficiency and without that CO2 you can't create the acid necessary to help balance the pH people have this idea that oxygen is the best thing in the world right CO2 is a waste product but you need a balance of both okay you don't want to just have one or the other if you have too much oxygen like even in oxygen therapy there are pretty major side effects especially if it's pure oxygen you can end up with edema swelling pulmonary edema it can be very dangerous because the CO2 is necessary to make the oxygen available to your cells and your tissues but the point is that when you see someone with alkalosis they're two alkaline they're breathing will be off and it will almost look like they're hyperventilating and then the last symptom for alkalosis would be like heart palpitations that type of thing now I've done another video on lemon juice that gets millions of views okay if you haven't checked that one out I put it up videos.title: Lemon Juice and Olive Oil: Benefits for Health

transcript.fullText: the health benefits of lemon juice and olive oil and a simple recipe to use at home extra virgin olive oil is one of the healthiest oils on the planet and is loaded with antioxidants and healthy fats it has been used for at least 5 000 years for its many health benefits and is a staple in the mediterranean diet lemon juice was also used by many ancient civilizations for purifying the body especially in the middle east it is still used today to strengthen the body against disease and for its wonderful flavor in cooking when these ingredients are mixed together they form an amazing remedy which has some fantastic health benefits these work wonders in cleansing the body of toxins nourishing the body and fighting diseases recipe you will need one half a tablespoon of olive oil and one half a tablespoon of lemon juice method mix the ingredients together in a small jar and shake well drink this once per day before a meal or on an empty stomach every morning before breakfast it is very important that you use extra virgin olive oil as this contains the antioxidants and phytonutrients that the body needs you should also use freshly squeezed raw lemon juice and avoid bottled varieties store-bought lemon juice is often pasteurized and does not contain the important vitamin c this mixture also works well as a salad dressing feel free to add a little stevia or raw honey for sweetness let's take a look at 17 benefits and uses of this healing mixture that you can enjoy when consuming it on a daily basis 1. kidney stones the citrates from the lemon juice help to prevent the formation of kidney stones it does this by helping the kidneys to flush out excess calcium and oxalates which come from plant-based foods olive oil works well in this mixture as it also helps you to absorb and digest minerals from vegetables 2. anti-aging the phenols in olive oil work with the vitamin c in lemon juice to slow the effects of aging on the body these are both packed full of antioxidants which fight oxidative damage to the dna three skin health drinking this mixture daily is wonderful for improving the health of the skin the vitamin c from lemons helps to produce collagen for strong healthy skin whilst the vitamin e from olive oil protects the skin from free radical damage together these can reduce blemishes pimples dark circles and age spots four heals arteries one of the main benefits of this mixture is its ability to help heal the artery walls from damage it is highly anti-inflammatory and can reduce the risk of arterial plaque and blood clots this reduces the risk of heart attacks strokes and embolisms naturally 5. varicose veins if you suffer with varicose veins or spider veins then drinking this daily can help lemon juice has strengthening tightening and anti-inflammatory effects on the vein walls you can also massage this mixture into the skin to help increase circulation and reduce pain 6. dandruff you can also apply this mixture directly to the scalp to fight off dandruff and flaky skin both ingredients are anti-fungal and antimicrobial meaning that they kill off any harmful microbes from the scalp and also help the skin to heal naturally seven absorb nutrients another fantastic benefit to this mixture is that it helps your body to absorb nutrients from other foods that you consume especially vegetables eating a salad with wild caught fish in cruciferous vegetables alongside this combination helps to nourish your cells with vitamins and minerals fast 8. inflammation olive oil contains oleocanthal and other antioxidants that have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body studies show that this is very effective at reducing pain and works similar to ibuprofen lemon juice also lowers inflammation especially when it comes to aching gums tooth pain and aching muscles 9. hair growth a mixture of lemon juice and olive oil is also used by many beauticians to promote hair growth applying this as a hair mask can strengthen the hair follicles which makes them less likely to break and split we also recommend adding a little warmed coconut oil to this to boost its effects 10 helps arthritis the alpha linoleic acid found in virgin olive oil has been linked to reducing arthritis pain and stiffness of the joints this has been shown to reduce inflammation especially for those with rheumatoid arthritis lemon juice helps your body to digest minerals which it can then use to strengthen the joints and connective tissues 11. furniture polish to use this mixture as a furniture polish simply add an extra half a tablespoon of olive oil massage this into wood furniture using a soft cloth following the grain of the wood wipe away excess liquid with a spare cloth be sure to test this in a small area to ensure that you are happy with the results 12. detoxification both of these nutrient-rich foods contain properties which help to detoxify the liver of harmful chemicals the antioxidants phenols and vitamins work hand in hand to cleanse the internal organs and strip away fat from the liver this improves your hormone balance energy levels and general well-being 13. healthy nails carefully apply this mixture to the nailed cuticle to help strengthen the nails helping them to grow stronger and healthier consuming this remedy daily orally will also help to build collagen and absorb more minerals for healthier nail growth 14. gallbladder stones the citric acid in the lemon juice helps to control the flow of bile from the liver and the gallbladder this can naturally reduce the risk of gallbladder stones and protect against inflammation in this organ research shows that people who consume lots of olive oil have a lower incidence of gallstones 15. skin whitening if you have dark patches of skin that you wish to lighten you can use this lemon juice and olive oil mixture to do this simply apply the mixture to the skin and scrub gently using a cotton pad repeat this process every two days until the area has lightened this works by gently exfoliating the skin and restoring its pigment with the citric acid and vitamin c sixteen nutrition this combination contains healthy doses of vitamin c oleic and linoleic acid palmitic acid vitamin k citric acid potassium folate and choline it is also rich in phytonutrients and antioxidants 17. brain health scientific studies show that consuming olive oil regularly can protect the brain against different forms of dementia the antioxidants and essential fatty acids in today's mixture helps to reduce inflammation in the structure of the brain as you can see lemon juice and olive oil is an amazing combination for human health especially when taken on an empty stomach or used as a salad dressing to learn more about natural remedies nutrition and healthy tonics please see our other videos thank you very much for listening a like is always appreciated and remember to subscribe and tap the bell for more healthy videos i wish you great health wealth and happiness

videos.title: When Lemons Are Cheap, I Do This Right Away — Lemons Stay Fresh and Good All Year Without Going Bad!

transcript.fullText: Every year when lemons are cheap, I buy a lot to use over time. But if you leave whole lemons in the fridge, they spoil very quickly. After trying many methods, I kept just three. They're easy to do and very practical for everyday cooking. Today, I'll share each step with you. The first method is one I've been using for over 10 years. With this method, I prepare everything in advance, so when I need it, it's ready right away. very convenient. First, I cut the lemons in half like this. Then, I squeeze them through a strainer so the seeds don't fall in and I don't have to pick them out later. As for the lemon peels, I'm curious, what do you usually do with them? A lot of people throw them away after juicing, but I don't. I keep them because lemon peels are actually very valuable. Most people just don't know how to use them properly. If you know how to prepare lemon peels the right way, you'll never throw them away again. I'll share that little secret with you at the end. Now, we have a bowl of fresh lemon juice. I pour the juice into an ice cube tray. Just a small amount in each cube, enough for one use. Then I place the tray in the freezer for about 1 to two hours until the lemon juice is frozen before moving on to the next step. Once frozen, I remove the cubes and put them into a zip top bag. This saves space and keeps the freezer organized. Stored this way, the lemon juice lasts about 10 months to a year. Perfect for stocking up when lemons are cheap and saving money. Where I live, a single lemon can cost up to $1.20. I also use a glass jar to store fresh lemon juice in the refrigerator where it keeps well for about 1 to two weeks. It's very convenient for daily cooking or making drinks anytime since using this method whenever I need lemon juice. I just drop one frozen cube straight into the pot or a glass of water. It melts quickly, the flavor stays fresh, and it never tastes bitter. And since then, my fridge no longer has half a lemon wrapped up and forgotten in the back corner. This second method is one I use almost every day. I keep the lemons whole and arrange them neatly in a ziploc bag, making sure they don't overlap. This helps prevent bruising. And it's very convenient to take out one lemon at a time when needed. After arranging them, I press out all the air and seal the bag tightly. This limits air exposure so the lemons don't lose moisture, the skin doesn't dry out, and the aroma stays intact. Then I place the entire bag of lemons in the freezer. When I need one, I take a lemon out and cut it in half. In my household, we usually use just half a lemon for one meal. I place that half into a bowl of warm water for about 2 to 3 minutes to let it thaw naturally. The remaining half goes back into the bag and straight into the freezer for the next use. This way, I don't have to worry about lemons becoming shriveled, dried out, or moldy like before. Thanks to this method, whenever lemons are cheap, I usually buy more to use over time, avoiding expensive lemons during the offse, and I've saved quite a bit of money over the years. Besides storing whole lemons in the freezer, there's another habit in my kitchen that I've kept for many years. It's this set of honey infused jars, and I use them almost every day. First, I slice the lemons into thin rounds. Cutting them thin helps the lemons absorb better, so the flavor releases more evenly when soaking. After that, I arrange the lemon slices into a glass jar like this. Do you have a glass jar at home? I only layer them gently without pressing too tightly so the lemons don't get crushed or release too much juice. I also leave a small gap of about 2 to 3 cm from the top of the jar. Once arranged, I pour in honey, just enough to completely cover the lemons. This method creates a mild sourness with a gentle sweetness from the honey, making it very easy to drink. In addition to this jar, I prepare two other small jars. One is ginger soaked in honey. I slice the ginger thinly and soak it the same way. The other jar is garlic soaked in honey. I only make a very small amount just for when it's needed. I seal all three jars tightly and place them in the refrigerator. After a few days, the lemon, ginger, and garlic begin to release their essence into the honey. The mixture thickens slightly and develops a gentle aroma. At this point, it's ready to use. When drinking, I scoop some lemon honey into a cup of warm water. Then, depending on the day, I add a little ginger honey or a very small amount of garlic honey. This way of using it is easier to drink, not too strong, and more suitable for different people compared to soaking everything together in one jar. On cold days or when my throat feels a bit dry or slightly irritated, drinking it this way feels much more soothing. This set of three jars is like a treasure to me. It's a simple habit that's easy to maintain, especially for older people like myself. After using them, I always seal the lids tightly and place the jars back in the refrigerator. This allows me to use them gradually throughout the cold season without worrying about spoilage or loss of aroma. The final part is the lemon peel. For me, lemon peel is very valuable, but most people tend to throw it away. Even though it's actually very useful, I usually grate the lemon peel very finely like this. Since I only need a small amount each time, I always prepare it in advance so it's ready whenever I cook without having to stop and do extra prep. After grating, I put the lemon zest into a ziploc bag and spread it out flat so it doesn't clump together and is easier to take out later. Then I seal the bag tightly and store it in the freezer. Whenever I need it, I simply take out a small amount and sprinkle it directly onto the dish without thawing. Just a little bit can completely change a dish. Whether it's baked goods, pasta, or pan seared chicken, frozen lemon zest still keeps its natural aroma, doesn't turn bitter, and is very convenient for cooking. Since using this method, I no longer waste lemon peels. And every time I'm in the kitchen, things feel much faster and easier. So, with just these four simple methods, whenever lemons are cheap, I only need to prepare them once and can use them over a long time, convenient, and without waste. Among the methods I've just shared, which one do you feel fits best with your daily cooking habits? If you have any other ways of using or storing lemons that you often apply in your kitchen, I'd really love to read about them in the comments. Thank you for watching until the end. I'll see you again in the next kitchen tips.

videos.title: How to Make Homemade Lemonade Using Real Lemons

transcript.fullText:
Today on The Stay At Home Chef I'm showing you how to make homemade lemonade using real lemons! Freshly Squeezed lemonade is an old-fashioned treat you can enjoy
all spring and summer long. I bought this huge bag of lemons at Costco and I cut
them all in half.  I'm going to take my strainer and put it directly over my measuring cup.
And squeeze the lemon juice right into it using my handy dandy little hand
juicer. The strainer will catch all of the seeds and pulp leaving
nothing but the juice.  I love this little hand juicer because it really digs
in there and get all of the juice out. When you're using fresh lemons it's hard
to predict how much lemon juice you'll get from each one so when you're making
homemade lemonade it's important to squeeze the lemon juice first because
homemade lemonade is all about the ratio. It looks like we have about 2
cups which is perfect. So now I'm going to pour this directly into a pitcher and
then head on over to the stove to make our simple syrup. We have a saucepan we'll get it heating here. And since we had two cups of lemon juice we will pour in 2 cups of water. You want to use the same ratio here and then 2 cups of sugar. You can see that the liquid is looks all milky. You want to start this over the heat until it goes clear again. This part

transcript.fullText: in this video we'll determine the ph of lemon we want to know the ph value of lemon juice so we have a fresh lemon here and we cut it in half and we're going to take the juice and figure out what the ph is so we have our scale here from 1 to 14. what we want to do is make sure the scale matches the ph paper we're using so first off we'll put some hydrochloric acid this is ph 0 we're using on the paper and it turns that red just like the scale next we have a ph of seven we're to drop that on the paper and as expected it's that green color ph 14 we're using sodium hydroxide put that on the paper there and we would expect to have a purple color very deep kind of purple blue color so our ph paper is working as expected now let's test lemon juice so we have a fresh piece of dry ph paper we drop that lemon juice on it and you see that we get that orange color if we compare that to our scale that orange color that's a ph of 3. if the lemon juice has a ph of 3 that's an acid i did test this with a ph meter and the value i came up with was 2.76 so pretty close to three so we can say pretty definitively that the ph of a lemon will be right around three probably depend on the type of lemon or the seeds and how old the lemon is that makes it an acid and that's actually why it tastes sour and for some recipes where you need to lower the ph most commonly we use lemon juice this is dr b and thanks for watching you

videos.title: Oil Treatments & Recipes : How to Treat Kidney Stones With Lemon Juice and Olive Oil

transcript.fullText: Hi, my name is Bob Lindy. I'm an acupuncturist and herbalist in St. Petersburg, Florida. Today, I'm going to talk to you about how to use olive oil and lemon juice in order to treat kidney stones. So, what we need to do is gather together a bowl to mix our ingredients in, a couple of lemons so that we can get up to uh 2 ounces of lemon juice and enough olive oil, ideally some extra virgin coldressed olive oil so we can get two ounces of the of the olive oil to mix in with it. One of the things that when we do use this technique, it's important to understand that traditionally this combination is actually used for gallbladder stones as opposed to kidney stones. In general, everything that I've read and experienced, this is not an effective treatment for kidney stones. In either type of stones, whether it's kidney stones or gallbladder stones, I always recommend that you work with a trained professional who will most likely recommend other options as far as how you go ahead and aggressively attack those stones. The biggest risk factor with this is if you have a large stone, whether it be a bladder or a gallbladder is that it get gets lodged and you end up with a back a backup of urine or that you block the biliary duct in the gallbladder in which case you'll lose your gallbladder or if you block the stone the stone blocks the urinary passageway you'll actually probably have to go to the hospital and get surgery. So, this is not something to do until you've actually had a doctor ascertain how big your kidney stones are to see whether you can safely pass them. In a perfect world, we avoid getting kidney stones altogether by eating fresh fruits and vegetables, lean meats, and drinking plenty of fresh, clean water. But let's go ahead and look at how this technique is done since you can commonly find information about this in a number of alternative health books as well in certain locations in the book. The first thing we want to do is to go ahead and cut our lemons and squeeze out 2 ounces of lemon juice. Put those into a bowl. And then we can measure out our 2 oz of olive oil and place those into the bowl. We mix those together. We can store those in the fridge. Once we add some water to that and we can drink up to 12 ounces a day throughout the day. It is very, very likely that this will also provide you with diarrhea. So, you might want to consume this when you're close to a bathroom so that you don't have any accidents. Should you lose the ability to pass urine, you get some abdominal bloating, or any pain in your abdomen, make sure that you contact a physician immediately. Should you get pain in your kidneys from this, acquire a fever, or feel a great deal of nausea, again, you should contact a medical medical professional immediately. So, if you're going to use this, make sure you do it in consultation with a medical professional. Do it carefully. Stay close to a bathroom. This is Bob Lindy in St. Petersburg with a lemon and olive oil treatment for kidney stones.

videos.title: How to Apply Lemon Juice on Skin : Skin Care Advice

transcript.fullText: [Music] hi I'm Jaclyn bavan a licensed aesthetician and skincare expert I'm here at Anor MedSpa in WN Creek California today's topic is how to apply lemon juice on your skin when life gives you lemons use them to get sexy glowing skin fight the signs of aging with vitamin C packed lemons the citric acid in lemons make it useful for exfoliating lightning blemishes and scars removing extra oil and a whole lot more wash the lemon and cut in half squeeze in a bowl and dilute with a bit of water dip a clean cotton ball into the juice and apply directly onto your blemish a few times during the day if you want to treat hyperpigmentation you can combine lemon juice with other fruit juices like papaya apple or even limes if you have sensitive skin combine lemon juice with honey or yogurt so you can keep it on your face longer wash your face and apply a moisturizer sunblock especially before going out into the sun to learn more about skincare remedies using lemon juice visit me on jacktv.com [Music]

segments.text: [Music]
hi I'm Jaclyn bavan a licensed
aesthetician and skincare expert I'm
here at Anor MedSpa in WN Creek
California today's topic is how to apply
lemon juice on your skin when life gives
you lemons use them to get sexy glowing
skin fight the signs of aging with
vitamin C packed lemons the citric acid
in lemons make it useful for exfoliating
lightning blemishes and scars removing
extra oil and a whole lot more wash the
lemon and cut in half squeeze in a bowl
and dilute with a bit of water dip a
clean cotton ball into the juice and
apply directly onto your blemish a few
times during the day if you want to
treat hyperpigmentation you can combine
lemon juice with other fruit juices like
papaya apple or even limes
if you have sensitive skin combine lemon
juice with honey or yogurt so you can
keep it on your face longer wash your
face and apply a moisturizer sunblock
especially before going out into the sun
to learn more about skincare remedies
using lemon juice visit me on jacktv.com
[Music]

videos.title: 6 Unbelievable Lemon Juice Uses for Plants

transcript.fullText: there are a lot of quirky ways to keep your green friends healthy and one of them is using lemon juice for plants it may sound a little weird but trust me it has its advantages watch till the end to learn all the amazing uses of lemon in the garden 1. weed treatment with lemon juice lemon juice can be used as an organic weed killer take four ounces of lemon juice and mix it with one quart of white vinegar fill a spray bottle and spray it on weeds it'll kill the wild growth by reducing the ph levels 2. boost germination rate according to a study done at the chinese academy of agricultural sciences lemon juice promotes cell division growth stimulates the activity of some enzymes and helps nutrients to transport to the parts of the plant more easily add three milliliters of lemon juice to a liter of water and use the solution to water the seeds at the time of sowing once they germinate you can water them again with this solution three balance ph levels of the soil any alkaline soil can be made favorable for acid loving plants using diluted lemon juice once a month for this add two tablespoons of lemon juice to a gallon of water and pour the solution into the base of the plant make sure you don't wet the leaves with it or else they'll burn 4. clean metal pots lemon juice is also a good choice to clean metal and copper pots the acidic content of it helps to eliminate deposits and hard water spots making them look new again add a few drops of lemon juice on a clean cloth and rub it on the pots for a few minutes before rinsing with water for best results 5. lemon peels for mosquitoes to keep mosquitoes away rub the lemon peels on the skin it'll leave a citrus scent on your body that mosquitoes hate you can also simmer the peels in water and spray the solution on mosquito infested areas 6. get rid of spiders lemon juice also works quite well in repelling the spiders away add 10 to 14 drops of lemon juice in a cup of water along with 8 to 10 drops of peppermint oil mix well and spray the solution around the spider's web you can also cut the lemon in half put some cloves in it and place it near the area that spiders infest to keep them at bay thanks for watching hit the like button and don't forget to subscribe for more interesting videos [Music]

videos.title: How to REMOVE DANDRUFF in 10 minutes USING LEMON JUICE!

transcript.fullText: HOW TO: get rid of dandruff fast with lemon juice| HairCare Hola guys! Today I'm going to show you how to get rid of dandruff really fast and very simple. I've being using lemon juice in my hair as a dandruff treatment for the past months and I can tell you from the first application how amazing this natural dandruff remedy work on my hair and how I felt instantly relief from this problem. For this natural treatment: you only need to have one or two fresh lemons cut it in half. First wet your hair with some water and when you're done squeeze the lemon juice right on your scalp: like this. Now massage your scalp with the tip of your fingers for five minutes. Lemon's acidity helps you to fight and prevent dandruff and bacteria, as a result you will be able to grow healthy hair, prevent hair loss and keep your scalp very clean. Wait another five minutes before washing your hair. By now, it will be ready to be rinse. Wash your hair as normal and at the end use cold water to seal those pores. Trying to use this simple homemade treatment once a week until you see improvement. Wash your hair at least three times per week to keep your scalp clean from oils. If you have oily hair avoid conditioner and hot air direct on your scalp brush your hair daily to promote more circulation. This is very important guys! Don't use lemon juice if your hair is dyed because it'll definitely wear the color off. Guys thank you so much for watching! If you like more videos like this Don't forget to hit the subscribe button for more Natural Beauty videos and please leave me a comment about natural treatments to fight dandruff. Pura Vida! 😘🇨🇷

natural dandruff remedy work on my hair
and how I felt instantly relief from
this problem.
For this natural treatment:
you only need to have one or two fresh
lemons cut it in half.
First wet your hair
with some water and when you're done
squeeze the lemon juice right on your
scalp: like this.
Now massage your scalp
with the tip of your fingers for five
minutes.
Lemon's acidity helps you to fight and
prevent dandruff and bacteria,
as a result you will be able to grow healthy hair,
prevent hair loss and keep your
scalp very clean.
Wait another five
minutes before washing your hair.
By now, it will be ready to be rinse.
Wash your hair as normal and at the end use cold
water to seal those pores.
Trying to use this simple homemade treatment once a week
until you see improvement.
Wash your hair at least three times per week to
keep your scalp clean from oils.
If you have oily hair
avoid conditioner and hot air direct on
your scalp
brush your hair daily to promote more
circulation.
This is very important guys!
Don't use lemon juice if your hair is
dyed because it'll definitely wear the color off.
Guys thank you so much for watching!
If you like more videos like this
Don't forget to hit the subscribe button for
more Natural Beauty videos and
please leave me a comment about natural
treatments to fight dandruff.
Pura Vida! 😘🇨🇷

videos.title: WHAT I DRINK EVERY MORNING for glowing skin, flat stomach | 디톡스 레몬진저샷 만들기

transcript.fullText:
This is your sign to start making 
your own wellness shots at home. It’s take about 10 minute, cost a couple 
of dollars and ingredients are so simple. I drink this shot to detox & debloat every time.
There is so many benefits like simply good for ur skin, anti inflammatory, reduce digestive 
issues, stronger immune system, boosts metabolism Starting with cutting lemon and ginger, 
peeling the skin of ginger is optional. Remove the lemon zest The next step is blend everything together 
with one cup of distilled water. For the next step we’re gonna strain it You should get the pure look what like this
The final step is filling the shot bottles. Im using a funnel to get the 
juice into the little bottles Also pour into an ice tray. you can use them 
in your herbal teas or make some fun mocktails. Store in the refrigerator 
and drink within 4 days

transcript.fullText: hello this is chef andros from nicosia cyprus uh today we are doing something um very simple but the question is not just how to make lemon juice we will talk a little bit about lemons generally now we have um elementary in the backyard these are beautiful um large lemons this is the variety that it makes very large lemons and very juicy these five lemons weighed close to a kilo and a half that is 300 grams each uh which is about 20 ounces i think um so what what are my utensils here i washed and cut my lemons in two i have a juicer i had a better one attached to my mixer but uh something went wrong so i'm using the old one i have which is slower but it will do the work i have a casserole collecting the juice just under the mixer i have a casserole and a sieve where i will strain my juice because i want it to be as clear as possible especially if you want to make a lemon squash you don't want any thick materials into the squash because it will turn bad very very soon so we do really need them a pure liquid out of the lemon um i am using um plastic bottles um from water that i bought from the supermarket and then i will show you if i attempt to buy were these these bottles empty they will cost me more than buying them full of water and the water i put it into a larger plastic of 10 liters and this one goes in the corner of my kitchen you can see i have a cold water dispenser that we normally use in summer now we don't use the refrigerator we just use the top uh to drink the water and of course we do buy purified water uh in larger or smaller containers but in this case um we will drink the water from the small bottles uh so we will use it we will not throw it away but still we will use new bottles for the juice and every year i am using a different brand of water so that if by chance we spare some bottles uh of of lemon juice we will consume the older one in the remaining three or four or five bottles or whatever so now i will continue i'm making my juice this is a very slow juicer but it will do the work uh it doesn't mind i'm not in a hurry we are in lockdown as you know i am retired and so um we have plenty of time so after i have squeezed a few lemons don't worry i will not do everything on camera because it will take precious time from the video but i will make just enough so that i will fill at least one or two bottles all right now why make our own lemon juice a since we can buy lemon juice from the supermarket well here in cyprus we are producing a lot of lemons and the cypriots consume a lot of lemon juice because we have the lemon tree in the backyard and we do like lemons we even squeeze on our kebabs we use it in cooking we use it for all purposes so what i will do now i am doing it on camera i will strain my juice through the sieve and a good way um is to one good way to strain it better is to do this we don't use a spoon into the sea we just hit a little bit this um and i will tell you on camera what we have collected this pulp i will not throw it away this one contains a lot of pectin and when i am making um lemon marmalade i will show you i have it just here when i make my my marmalade uh it's this is it there is a lot of pectin actually this one you can read onto the description it says lemon honey ginger so this rather orange color is from the ginger and the honey but this special patch it was with this recipe the restaurant will be just lemons and sugar and i will utilize this um pulp that comes out of the segments of the lemons because it will stiffen it will set my marmalade much sooner so right now i have collected some juice and i will start filling my bottles but be careful what i will do i will only fill one bottle because there isn't really much juice to make more now when we fill liquids that we will freeze we do not feel the bottle to the top of the neck we leave it two or three centimeters one inch one inch and a half lower so that when we freeze it i will show you nearer than the camera so we put the lid and we close it tightly so you see i left my my juice much lower because liquids tend to expand when they freeze but then i will do something else i will just release the cup a little bit squeeze it a little bit like that and close it i still leave some some air on top okay so when when i put it into into my at deep freeze when it expands it will fill the bottle again so it will not burst into the refrigerator into the difference and this can keep for a very very long time into the deep freeze so why use our own lemon juice why not buy from the supermarket now um in cyprus it's uh it's very funny to listen that some someone buys juice from the supermarket because we have lemons uh in most months of the year and then we can squeeze and keep some in our deep freeze for this season that we don't have enough lemon juice now if you see a colored water in the supermarket writing lemon juice but it does not have this color it's just um a transparent yellow liquid i am afraid it is not lemon juice it is citric acid and some color and some water well for the secret people that is unacceptable because so it does not have the aroma it does not have the taste of lemon juice it can still do the work and uh some people who want to cheat um in restaurants that they need a lot of lemon juice probably they would buy the citric acid and produce their own juice without paying a lot of money so i turn the camera on me now so hello again this is chef andros hello today my cameraman did a better job and this is i decided not to fire him so he will keep um rolling the videos for me so the purpose of this video is that um simple things uh can give you better quality healthier options so we don't use chemicals although citric acid is not proved to be harmful and especially if it is made from lemons because they can extract citric acid from lemons the same way that they can extract the acetic acid from vinegar but for vinegar we will talk uh some other time when i when i make pickles but uh today it was so just about the lemons um so um for my home i have a deep frizz in my kitchen that's it it's about one a yard three a feet wide it's about 90 centimeters high it's about 55 centimeters deep and then i keep um i did tell you before that the restaurant owners you know they have some bad habits that they cannot get rid of so one of the habits is to have too many refrigerators at home one for the water uh one for you as a deep freezer another one for the salads we even have another one outside keeping other stuff especially when we have guests so um it is a very good idea if you are living in a in an area that the lemons they are cheap uh you can buy a lot of lemons make your juice in our home i we are i will make about 50 small bottles that is 25 liters and this is enough for all the here and because um during the winter months we do have french lemons that we can collect from the tree and then use it but as from october or rome uh excuse me as from march or or april um lemons are not of very good quality except if the variety is the one that reproduces lemons all the year round but this special lemon that i showed to you you see it's very big all right and very juicy so this variety it produces only once a year and we can collect our lemons for maybe two or three months every year the rest of the months we are using the lemon juice we have pure and healthy lemon juice i have another video how to make lemon squash okay to make lemon squash you put this quantity with this quantity of sugar you will stir it thoroughly even you can put it into your blender and blend it together and you make lemon juice a lemon squash uh and that is one of the offerings that you will get as soon as you arrive in cyprus if you come from abroad the first thing that you will notice is that somebody has got a tray full of glasses filled with chilled lemon squash to offer to you so thank you very much for watching this is chef andros jaralampus keep in touch you know it's the corona year in season we are locked home we have nothing else to do but it's very nice to communicate with you bye-bye thank you

videos.title: Morning Detox Secret: Lemon Water & Himalayan Salt Benefits for Digestion & Immunity

transcript.fullText: namaskar the most important thing to make a successful life for yourself is the Habit you follow so today I will share with you one transformative habit you can do every morning before 900 a.m. for a full body detox The Habit I'm talking about is drinking a glass full of lemon water with a pinch of Himalayan salt lemon water should be B this should be taken before 9:00 a.m. in the morning this sounds like something very small but this small practice can have tremendous amount of benefit to your health and definitely well-being now let's understand why warm lemon water with Himalayan salt is so powerful first it detoxes your body at cellular level Himalayan salt mixed with lemon juice and water and Himalayan salt helps us to pull toxins from your each cell reducing cellular toxicity this will reduce your risk from various chronic diseases next is liver support see lemons are rich in vitamin C and antioxidant lemon contains about 53 mg of vitamin C per 100 G of juice this is more than what Apple raspberry or mangoes provide Lon supports your liver detox Pathways they also stimulates bile production helping your body process toxins more efficiently third is electrolyte balance Himalayan pink salt contains trace minerals like magnesium potassium and calcium which help balance your body's electrolytes this helps with hydration and also improves your energy level and fourth important point is improved digestion warm water soothes your digestive system and helps flush out toxins lemons are excellent for fighting inflammation lemons can help dissolve the uric acid in your joints also helps repairing your tendons ligaments and Bones lemon is also excellent for tissue repair now let's see how to make it the ingredients you need are one cup of warm water water should be filtered water and not boiling water juice of a half fresh fresh lemon a pinch of Himalayan pink salt now the steps to make this lemon drink are warm the water but ensure it's not too hot it should be comfortable to sip then squeeze juice of half lemon and add a small pinch of saala and Pig salt and stir it well sip slowly and mindfully this one habit will take less than 5 minutes but its benefit will last for whole day now let's see why it is so important to do this before 900 a.m. first it keeps your energy level high throughout the day lemon is full of vitamin C and Himalayan salt has more than 80 trace minerals like sodium potassium magnesium Etc these trace minerals are very important for body's functions like muscle contraction nerve impulse and fluid balance when you have this drink before 9:00 a.m. you are giving a body a Rel of this vital nutrients so your body is recharged nourished and you will have more energy for high intensity activity whole day next it gives your body a powerful immunity this liquid helps your entire system to immune better so when you have it early in the morning you give your body the immunity boost it helps you protect from pathogens throughout the day then hydration and Metabolism boost you see hydration is very important for the body when you have lemon water early in the morning it takes care of your body's dehydration which suffer throughout the night having lemon also helps your body to absorb nutrients so when you have lemon water with himan salt before 9 whatever you eat later is processed better and your body can absorb the nutrients faster the combination of Himalayan salt and lemon water also helps you to recover from exercise faster and reduces the risk of muscle craps so have this drink before 9 to give you a protective cover throughout the day finally there is this morning intention and intentional tone for the rest of the day it shows that you are being mindful about your health and well-being so make it a part of your morning ritual and you will noce that how you are different you feel energetic and refreshed right from morning but also keep in mind if you have acid reflex or sensitive teeth consider using a straw to drink lemon water to minimize contact with your teeth the key to detoxification isn't just one habit It's a combination of healthy choices throughout the day including a balanced diet exercise and Pro for rest so do this one thing every day before 900 a.m. to help your body detox throughout the day try it for a few days and see how you feel so stay hydrated stay healthy and feel that good feeling in the morning start with this lovely feeling namaskar

videos.title: Fresh lemon juice soap recipe - a natural citric acid and colour boost! (full tutorial)

transcript.fullText:
This video has been on my list to make for ages. 
And then my husband comes up from the backyard last weekend with this basket full of juicy Meyer 
lemons off our tree. So, the time is perfect to share this video and soap recipe with you. Today, 
I'm making cold processed soap with lemon juice, which is quite fun, a bit of an adventure, 
and also really useful because lemon juice has a good amount of citric acid in it. So, it's 
an alternative to adding citric acid powder to your soap recipes. Fresh juices from ripe fruit 
like these are also full of natural sugars which help to boost the lather of your soap recipes. 
If you're new here, hi, my name is Elly and I share tutorials and recipes across two channels 
on YouTube, Elly's Everyday Soap Making and Elly's Everyday Wholegrain Sourdough. If you're 
a beginner here, please check out the description below this video where I'll share links to my 
beginner tutorials and everything else related to this recipe video. So, why add citric acid 
to your soap recipes? Citric acid in soap acts as a chelating agent binding with the metal ions 
present in hard water, which reduces soap scum and helps slow down the oxidization of soap making 
oils, which ultimately extends the shelf life of your soap. I find this really useful because I'm 
often using up old bottles of oil that I'm given or that I've had around for a while. Ideally, 
we'd all use super fresh oil in our soap recipes, but I know that's not the reality for many of 
us. And I'd rather make the most of what I have rather than waste precious resources. Whether I'm 
using the skin of the lemons or just the juice, I always wash my fruit before processing it. 
Citrus fruit can hang on the tree for a long time before it's harvested, so it's good to 
clean them well with a good scrub under fresh water. I thought I might use some of the zest of 
these lemons in my soap, so I grated some of it, but you'll see that later I didn't end up using 
it, and I'll explain why not when we get to that part. The most important thing I needed to extract 
from these lemons was their juice. And wow, my lemons were so ripe and soft. They were absolutely 
full of juice. If you've got hard unripe lemons, just leave them out for a week or two and let them 
ripen. You'll know they're ripe when they've got a bit of softness to them. You'll get a lot more 
juice out of them that way, and they'll also have more sugars in them. If you're using Meyer lemons 
like these, their juice is about 4% citric acid by weight. So, you'll need 315 grams of Meyer lemon 
juice for this recipe to achieve 1% citric acid of the base soap oils used in the recipe. If you're 
using regular lemon varieties, not meyer lemons, they have more citric acid in them. So, you'll 
need only 250ml/250g of fresh lemon juice. Don't worry though, I'll put all of these little 
details in the written post on my website. This recipe only applies to fresh lemon juice, 
not store-bought bottled juice. And it's really important to know that when you add citric acid to 
soap recipes, the lye amount needs adjusting. So, please do your homework and calculate a new 
recipe for yourself, unless you have meyer lemons and use the same oils that I used in this 
recipe. Once you have your lemon juice squeezed, strain out any pulp and measure 315 ml or 315 g 
of the juice into a jug. We need a total of 355 g of liquid for this recipe, but only 315 g of 
juice to get the right citric acid amount. So, we need to add 40 g of water to bring the total 
liquid amount to 355 g. If you're using non Meyer lemon type lemon juice, you need 250 g of fresh 
juice with 105 g of water to make the 355 g total. Just add the water to the juice and stir them to 
combine. Then pop that into an ice cube tray and freeze it for a few hours until it's solid. We'll 
be using this iced juice mixture to make the lye solution for the soap. Next, I start preparing my 
other ingredients. I'm still trying to use up my stash of palm oil. So, I'm using 441 g of that in 
this recipe. Please don't berate me for using palm oil. I've got my reasons and I've made videos 
about it to explain that if you're interested. I'm also just trying to use it up. So, waste not 
want not. You can also use tallow or lard in place of palm oil if you have those available to you. 
They will work great in this recipe. In with the palm oil, I add 315 gram of coconut oil, which I 
had sitting out in the sun to melt because it's quite cool here. Palm fruit and coconut oils are 
the two hard fats I'm using in the recipe. So, I put them together into one jug and very, very 
gently melted them together in the microwave. I know there are a lot of microwave haters out 
there, but I honestly cannot find any evidence whatsoever to suggest that there's anything wrong 
with doing this. Just use a low power setting. Go really slow and low heat and stir it frequently 
as you go and it will just be a lovely gentle melting method. While those are melting together, 
I measure my liquid oils. And for this recipe, I'm using a simple combination of canola and 
castor oil. For the canola, we'll need 441 g, which is the same amount as the palm oil. And 
for castor oil, we need only 63 g. Next, I put my gloves and my safety goggles on and weighed out 
the sodium hydroxide, which will become the lye solution that turns my oils into soap. Please know 
what you're doing when handling this ingredient. It's highly corrosive and you really must protect 
your eyes and your skin at all times when making soap from scratch. Carefully weigh 185 gram of 
sodium hydroxide into a container. Put the lid on it straight away and place it somewhere secure 
until you're ready to make the soap. This sodium hydroxide amount has been adjusted to account for 
the citric acid in my lemon juice which consumes some of the lye. You'll notice in the bottom left 
corner of the screen that I've got a little bowl of dried calendula flowers there. I didn't end 
up using those, so please ignore that. Next, I prepared my essential oils, which are totally 
optional, but I wanted to add some to this recipe. For a fresh lemony scent, I used 9 g of lemon 
myrtle essential oil, 10 g of litsea cubea, and 12 g of cedar atlas essential oil. The lemon 
juice doesn't give any fragrance to this soap and lemon essential oil isn't well suited for soap 
recipes. So, these are good alternatives that you can use in small amounts or you can just leave the 
soap unscented. It will still be really beautiful. Now that I have all of my ingredients ready, 
the last thing is to prepare my workstation, get my molds ready, and wait for my lemon juice 
ice cubes to be fully frozen. I started off with a beautiful sunny winter's day on this day, but then 
the clouds came over, so I lost my nice lighting, but never mind. A bit of a extra work light saved 
the day. And once everything was ready to go, it was time to make the soap. The first step is 
to put the lemon juice ice cubes into a jug to make the lye solution. Making sure you've got 
your safety goggles and your gloves still on. Carefully pour the sodium hydroxide granules over 
the ice and start mixing. The ice cubes will melt as they start reacting with the lye and eventually 
you'll have a golden orange colored lemon juice lye solution. It was such an amazing color that it 
was at this point where I decided not to use the grated lemon zest or the calendula petals that I'd 
prepared. I really just wanted to see the color of this soap without them added. And I'm glad I 
chose that option because they looked so nice in the end. And yeah, it was just good to see what 
they'd be like just with the lemon juice. Once my lye solution was mixed, I added my melted hard 
fats into my liquid fats or my liquid oils and stirred them together. It's totally optional, but 
I decided to add in a little bit of white kaolin clay. So, I put a heaped teaspoon of that into 
the oils and blended it through thoroughly. Then, I did a quick temperature check and I was very 
happy to see that both my lye solution and my oils were perfect at around 40° C, which is around 
about 102 F. You don't want your oils or your lye solution to be too hot or your soap could 
thicken up too quickly. To mix the soap batter, I carefully poured my lye solution into the oils 
using a strainer to filter out any small beads of undissolved lye, which is really important 
when you're using ice for your lye solution. And because of the palm oil and coconut oil in this 
recipe, along with the sugars in the lemon juice, I blended this batch only for a couple of 
short bursts with my stick blender to begin the emulsification and then I hand stirred it the 
rest of the way. If you're using individual molds, you want to keep this batter as fluid as you can 
for as long as you can. So, try not to over blend it. I kept a really close eye on this and as soon 
as I could feel it thickening, I quickly added the essential oil, mixed that through thoroughly, 
and thankfully my molds were laid out on boards ready to go. So, I grabbed them as I stirred and 
then poured the soap. I poured the soap batter back into my large jug, and that made it a lot 
easier. Slowly and carefully, I poured the soap batter into the molds. This batch made almost 18 
full flower soaps, which was perfect for my three molds of six soaps each. I love this part of soap 
making. That moment when you realize you mixed it just enough, but not too much that it got too 
thick to pour. You could absolutely make this soap in log molds, too, which would be easier, but not 
as cute, I don't think. I really love these flower soap molds. As many of you probably know. My soap 
was starting to thicken by the end of the pour, but I just even the bars out as best as I could. 
I gave some of the thicker ones a little spiral swirl with my chopstick to even out the lumps, and 
I think they came out pretty good. The final touch was to spray these with alcohol, which helps 
reduce soda ash on the bars. Then I put them into my slightly warm oven for a little while. I 
eventually took them out of the oven and put them in my large cooler wrapped in a blanket overnight 
because I needed the oven. This often happens to me, which is generally why I use the cooler. And 
this is where things got interesting. I'm so used to making simple soaps in log molds that saponify 
quite quickly, usually within the first day or so. So, I was a bit dismayed when I took these 
out a day later and discovered that they were still really caustic and hadn't yet saponified. I 
freaked out a bit to be honest since I'd spent the whole day the day before calculating, preparing, 
and filming the recipe. I honestly thought I'd made a mistake in my calculations or maybe my 
lemon juice had a lot less citric acid in it than I thought. So, I was a bit disappointed. But then 
I thought for a bit and realized that it might be okay. It might just need more time. And that's 
what turned out to be the case. It was probably the fact that it's winter here. The soap batter 
was not very warm and not very well insulated in the individual molds, which can slow down the 
saponification process. And you know, perhaps the citric acid slows things down a bit too, even 
though I did adjust the lye amount. I'm not sure, but some soap recipes do take longer to saponify, 
particularly in individual molds where there's not much gelling happening. I felt a bit more hopeful 
after taking out one of the bars on the first day, and I hot processed it in the microwave, which 
is a nice quick way to see if the soap recipe is okay or not. Thankfully, that test bar did 
completely saponify after I hot processed it. So, I felt confident that the rest of the batch 
would be okay in a couple of days time. And here is the soap 3 days later on Monday morning. 
All of the bars were smooth and fully saponified, and they tested perfect with my pH test strips. 
So, it worked. The only caveat I'd say about this recipe now is that you never really know how much 
citric acid exactly is in your lemon juice. So, it's a bit of a gamble. But I think if you 
calculate a decent super fat into your recipe, at least 5%, maybe even up to 8 or 9%, I 
did 5%. Then you should be okay. Either way, I am very happy with this soap. It smells great. 
It looks wonderful. It helped me to use up some more of my old palm oil and canola oils that I've 
been trying to use up bit by bit in each new video recipe that I share. And it's a lovely soap with 
a rather nice lather, too. I used the little hot processed bar to test the lather. And yeah, it's a 
really, really nice soap. I love the way it feels. Have you tried lemon juice in your soap recipes 
or other citrus juices? How did you calculate them? How did they go? I'd love to hear from you 
in the comments if you want to chat with me there. And don't forget to see the description for all 
of the resources you will need for this recipe. Have a brilliant day. Thanks for all of 
your support and I'll see you again soon.
